query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: A black and white photograph of a long, symmetrical institutional hallway with a polished reflective floor, exposed brick columns, painted cinder block walls, and a suspended ceiling with rectangular fluorescent light panels. Wooden doors are evenly spaced along both walls, a folding chair rests against the left wall near the foreground, and double doors are visible at the far end of the corridor beneath a receding line of overhead lights.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A black and white photograph of a long, symmetrical institutional hallway with a polished reflective floor, exposed brick columns, painted cinder block walls, and a suspended ceiling with rectangular fluorescent light panels. Wooden doors are evenly spaced along both walls, a folding chair rests against the left wall near the foreground, and double doors are visible at the far end of the corridor beneath a receding line of overhead lights. The empty, starkly lit space evokes atmospheric distress, producing a liminal mood of isolation and unease.
